question_text: A student measures a regular rectangular block and finds it has a mass of 240 g and dimensions of 10 cm × 8 cm × 3 cm. Calculate the density of the block in g/cm³.
explanation: First, calculate the volume: 10 cm × 8 cm × 3 cm = 240 cm³. Then use the density formula: density = mass ÷ volume = 240 g ÷ 240 cm³ = 1.0 g/cm³. This density is the same as water, which makes sense since this block would neither float nor sink in water.
